source_note: Management & Operations Audit of UGI Utilities, Inc. (incl. UGI Central Penn Gas and UGI Penn Natural Gas), PA PUC Bureau of Audits — cover 'Issued October 2019', Docket Nos. D-2018-3002234, D-2018-3002235, D-2018-3002236; 45 recommendations across the Exhibit I-2 table (the 2023 MEI follow-up tracked a 23-rec significant subset). PDF: puc.pa.gov/pcdocs/1644014.pdf. Cover prints only the month, so issued_date is left null. source_page_url is the 2023 MEI follow-up (the original 2019 announcement is not in the current archive). Findings parsed verbatim from Exhibit I-2.

recommendations.text: Improve the maintenance and tracking of safety training through automation.

Periodically review spans of control for UGI Utilities’ management positions and document justification for supervisory position ratios with narrow or wide spans of control and adjust reporting relationships as appropriate.
Centralize policy management and establish requirements to periodically review and update them.
Organize the Internal Audit function to report administratively to the UGI Corp. CEO, another non- financial senior officer of UGI Corp., or directly to the Board of Directors.
Revise UGI Utilities’ delegation of authority policy to strengthen governance controls for the new EVP Natural Gas position.
Create a periodic, recurring process to compare the internal cost of services provided between UGI Utilities and its affiliates to market rates.
Update UGI Corp.’s Cost Allocation Manual.
Work with UGI Corp. to improve the level of detail provided in intercompany invoices and reports.
Develop time entry training to address direct charging of labor for intercompany services.
Amend or file new affiliated interest agreements to more accurately detail services provided.
Conduct periodic internal audits of affiliate transactions and shared service costs.
Document or update existing policies or procedures to reflect actual practices.
Continue to reduce the number of third-party damages and increase the damage collection rate at UGI North.
Decrease the inventory of outstanding Class A leaks and continue to reduce the number of main leaks at UGI North.
Maintain focus on safety with the objective of meeting or exceeding safety KPI goals.
Continue accelerated efforts to identify and safely remove and dispose of all mercury regulators in UGI South’s service territory.
Follow through on plans to develop a centralized training facility to aid in OQ training
Consider redefining emergent and non-emergent work and train supervisors on the usage of overtime and continue to work to reduce overtime that is not related to an emergency.
Establish and track response times by subcategories and take corrective action to continue improving response times for emergency outage calls.
Investigate the costs and related benefits of implementing some EMS features into the existing SCADA system.
Install sprinkler systems at all regularly occupied facilities, where feasible.
Establish physical security standards that apply at company facilities and devote resources to adequately maintain oversight.
Update the PSP to include relevant physical security efforts and review, update, and test it annually.
Establish a clear chain of command for physical security by placing the Director of Physical Security in charge of all physical security at UGI Utilities.
Perform penetration tests, risk analysis, and vulnerability assessments of physical security routinely, and periodically utilize external/independent resources.
Improve the CSP and relevant procedures, secure all PII, and consider formalizing policies at the UGI Corporation level.
Complete the gas SCADA communication upgrade.
Review and adjust resources to address the business continuity program’s deficiencies.
Continue improvement of the ERP, and address deficiencies in associated training.
Maintain up to date printed copies of SDS near where chemicals are stored or in a relevant control center.
Improve company-wide inventory turnover and exclude emergency stock from inventory turnover calculations.
Automate the materials management function.
Develop a detailed material procurement manual and track vendor performance.
Improve cycle count accuracy rates at warehouses in the UGI Central division.
Ensure forward-looking goals and objectives are fully defined within the IT Strategic Plan.
Expand the required IT certifications to drive wanted skill competencies.
Integrate the IT Department as part of the procurement process to review and evaluate cybersecurity issues for communications-capable operations equipment.
Reduce long-term accounts receivable balances.
Resolve integration issues between the new customer information system and program used to support billing and collections processes for UGI Utilities’ Customer Assistance Program.
Improve customer service performance metrics.
Improve the retention of Customer Care Representatives within the Customer Information Center.
Determine optimal vehicle and equipment replacement strategy and update vehicle replacement guidelines.
Develop key performance indicators tailored to effectively manage UGI Utilities’ fleet, reduce costs, and drive efficiency.
All documentation provided to new hires should be reviewed periodically and updated as necessary.
Prepare and file annual diversity reports in accordance with Commission guidelines for annual PUC diversity filing.
